meaning: To forget about time passing while doing something
example: I was so absorbed in the book that I lost track of time.

meaning: To have no more time left to do something
example: We ran out of time before finishing the exam.

meaning: To maintain regular communication with someone
example: Let's keep in touch after you move away.

meaning: To stop communicating with someone over time
example: We lost touch after graduating from college.

meaning: To reach a point where you cannot make further progress
example: After weeks of study, I felt like I had hit a wall.

meaning: To permanently damage a relationship or opportunity
example: Don't burn bridges with former colleagues; you may need them later.

meaning: To use one's influence or connections to get something done
example: He had to pull strings to get tickets to the sold-out show.

meaning: To stop working for the rest of the day
example: We've done enough for now â€” let's call it a day.

meaning: To alter your opinion or decision
example: She changed her mind about studying abroad.

meaning: To decide firmly on something
example: Please make up your mind before the offer expires.

meaning: To wait in suspense; or literally to stop breathing temporarily
example: Everyone held their breath as the winner was announced.

meaning: To formally request to be considered for a position
example: He applied for a job at the leading tech company.

meaning: To accept or become comfortable with a difficult situation
example: It took years to come to terms with losing his sight.

meaning: To remove or eliminate something you no longer want
example: It's time to get rid of all these old clothes.

meaning: To make good use of an opportunity
example: Take advantage of the sale while it lasts.

meaning: To execute or implement a strategy or scheme
example: The team carried out the plan flawlessly.

meaning: To invest time and energy into doing something
example: If you put in the effort, the results will follow.

meaning: To carefully notice and be precise about small things
example: This job requires you to pay attention to detail.

meaning: To share the same interests, qualities, or features
example: Despite their differences, they have a lot in common.

meaning: To look after or be responsible for someone or something
example: She took care of her elderly parents devotedly.

meaning: To be first or in charge; to guide others
example: She decided to take the lead on the new project.

meaning: To learn how to do something through practice
example: Once you get the hang of it, it becomes much easier.

meaning: To make something easier to understand; to clarify
example: The investigation shed light on the cause of the accident.

meaning: To begin to doubt a decision you have already made
example: She had second thoughts about quitting her job.
